channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Supreme Court observes that Centre may need to act on Punjab’s drug crisis
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has highlighted the worsening drugs problem in Punjab and observed that the intervention of the Central Government may be necessary to tackle the issue effectively. The benc...

item.title: Prime Video Launches Short-Form Video Clips Feed for Easier Content Discovery
item.description: Amazon has introduced a new short-form video feed called “Clips” within its Prime Video app, initially rolling out in the United States. The feature showcases short snippets of Prime Video content, de...

item.title: AMMK Chief Questions Legitimacy of MLA Support Claimed by TVK in Tamil Nadu Elections
item.description: During the recent Tamil Nadu election results, a controversy has emerged regarding the claims made by TVK about the support of an AMMK MLA. The AMMK chief has informed NDTV that the assertion by TVK t...

item.title: Bombay HC directs Maharashtra to provide financial aid to children’s homes alongside Ladki Bahin scheme
item.description: The Aurangabad bench of the Bombay High Court has clarified that the Maharashtra government cannot deny or delay financial assistance to children’s homes while extending support to underprivileged wom...

item.title: Pentagon Releases Previously Unseen UFO Files, Including Historical Space Race Documents
item.description: The Pentagon has released a collection of previously unseen files on unidentified flying objects, sparking renewed public interest in mysterious aerial phenomena. The release includes documents from t...

item.title: Labour loses Birmingham City Council as multiple parties and independents gain seats
item.description: Labour has lost control of Birmingham City Council after 14 years in power, facing one of its most severe defeats in recent memory. The party's seat count dropped dramatically from 65 in 2022 to fewer...

item.title: Disagreement Among APC Governors Over PGF Leadership, Uzodimma Still Chairman
item.description: One kind wahala don burst for Progressive Governors Forum (PGF) as tori don dey say 20 governors of All Progressives Congress (APC) don pass vote of no confidence against the forum chairman, Imo State...

item.title: Global Health Authorities Track Passengers Following Hantavirus Outbreak on MV Hondius
item.description: Health authorities worldwide are urgently tracking passengers and crew from the MV Hondius cruise ship after a hantavirus outbreak was detected. Five cases have been confirmed, including three deaths,...

item.title: Supreme Court urges speedy completion of Lakhimpur Kheri 2021 violence trial
item.description: The Supreme Court of India expressed strong concern over the slow pace of proceedings in the 2021 Lakhimpur Kheri violence case, in which Ashish Mishra, son of former Union minister Ajay Mishra, is an...

item.title: Constitutional Court invalidates National Assembly vote on Phala Phala impeachment
item.description: In a landmark decision on Friday, the Constitutional Court of South Africa declared that Rule 129(i)(b) of the National Assembly rules was inconsistent with the Constitution and therefore invalid. The...

item.title: Les Kiss assesses refereeing mistakes in Queensland Reds' loss to Chiefs
item.description: Queensland Reds coach Les Kiss offered a measured assessment following his team's defeat to the Chiefs, focusing on the impact of several officiating errors during the match. While the Reds fought har...

item.title: itel Energy Promotes Solar Solutions to Reduce Blackouts and High Energy Costs
item.description: If you dey tired of constant blackouts, high fuel costs, noisy generators, and sharp increase in your electricity bills, itel Energy don bring solution. For Power and Water Exhibition 2026, dem showca...

item.title: Understanding How Teens Contribute to Society Beyond Traditional Civic Activities
item.description: Contrary to popular belief, many teenagers are actively engaged in contributing to society, though not always in ways that adults traditionally recognize. Research involving 723 American adolescents f...

item.title: Nelson Mandela Bay Temporarily Halts Burials at Flooded Cemeteries for Safety Reasons
item.description: The Nelson Mandela Bay Municipality has temporarily suspended burials at a number of its municipal cemeteries following recent heavy rainfall and flooding. The municipality stated that this measure is...

item.title: Air India defers annual salary increments amid rising costs and airspace challenges
item.description: Air India has decided to defer its employees' annual salary increments by at least one quarter as part of cost-cutting measures in response to current economic uncertainties. The decision was communic...

item.title: Covered Bridge Potato Chip addresses community concerns in Woodstock, N.B.
item.description: Covered Bridge Potato Chip, a well-known snack company in Woodstock, New Brunswick, is taking steps to address concerns raised by local residents about noise, smells, and increased traffic near its fa...

item.title: Donny Pangilinan and Belle Mariano Actively Contribute to ‘Tayo Sa Wakas’ Soundtrack
item.description: Donny Pangilinan and Belle Mariano, two of the Philippines' most beloved young actors, have taken a very active role in the creation of the soundtrack for the TV series ‘Tayo Sa Wakas’. The duo not on...

item.title: Man convicted for 1992 attempted rape after DNA breakthrough
item.description: A 56-year-old man, Aron Traynor, has been sentenced to 13 years in prison for attempting to rape a 74-year-old woman in Stockport, Greater Manchester, in 1992. At the time of the attack, police were u...

item.title: Tamil Nadu Governor Awaits Complete Support Letters Before Inviting TVK to Form Government
item.description: The Tamil Nadu Governor's office has not yet sent an invitation to Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) chief C Joseph Vijay to form the state government, as all supporting letters have not been received. T...

item.title: Manitoba Adds Safety Officers to Grace Hospital Following Staff Assaults
item.description: Manitoba’s provincial government has announced that additional institutional safety officers will be deployed at Winnipeg’s Grace Hospital following a recent incident where three staff members were se...
